label: Standard — tech podcast inviting AI researcher

input.guest_background: AI safety researcher who recently published a paper on alignment benchmarks for large language models. She runs a research lab at a university and has been vocal about the gap between corporate AI safety claims and actual practice.
input.show_description: A weekly podcast about AI and machine learning for technical professionals. Audience: software engineers, ML practitioners, and tech leaders.
input.why_this_guest: Her new paper directly challenges the methodology behind several prominent AI safety benchmarks, which our audience uses and trusts
input.specific_hook: Whether the benchmarks the industry relies on are actually measuring what they claim to measure
input.reference_work: Her paper 'Benchmarking the Benchmarks: A Critical Assessment of LLM Safety Evaluations'

input.guest_background: A veteran freelance photographer who has been shooting editorial assignments for 20 years and recently started teaching workshops on building a sustainable freelance career
input.show_description: A podcast about making a living in creative fields. Audience: freelance creatives and aspiring full-time artists.
input.why_this_guest: His experience bridging the gap between artistic ambition and financial sustainability is exactly what our listeners struggle with

label: European context — UK politics podcast inviting EU policy expert

input.guest_background: German political scientist at a Brussels-based think tank specializing in EU-UK relations post-Brexit. She has published extensively on trade policy and regulatory divergence between the EU and UK.
input.show_description: A twice-weekly podcast analyzing UK politics and policy for an engaged, politically literate audience. 11,000 downloads per episode.
input.why_this_guest: The upcoming EU-UK trade review is the most significant policy moment in the relationship since the Trade and Cooperation Agreement, and she is one of the few analysts who understands both sides
input.specific_hook: What the EU actually wants from the trade review versus what the UK government thinks they want — and why the gap matters
input.reference_work: Her recent policy brief 'The Review That Could Reset EU-UK Relations' published by the European Policy Centre
input.connection: She appeared on a sister podcast in the network last year and our producer has her contact details
input.notable_recent_guests: Former UK trade negotiators, European Commission officials, senior Westminster journalists

input.guest_background: Award-winning Ghanaian-British chef who runs two Michelin-starred restaurants in London and has a Netflix series. She is vocal about decolonising fine dining and has pushed back against the term 'ethnic food.'
input.show_description: A food and culture podcast exploring how cuisine reflects identity, migration, and power. 22,000 downloads per episode.
input.why_this_guest: Her new cookbook explicitly rejects the Western fine dining canon and builds a new framework rooted in West African culinary tradition
input.specific_hook: Why she returned her Michelin stars — and what she thinks should replace the rating system
input.reference_work: Her cookbook 'The New Table' and her Guardian long-read about the politics of fine dining
input.connection: No prior contact — this is a cold outreach. Her publicist is known to be protective and selective.

input.guest_background: Economist at the Indian Statistical Institute, Kolkata. Leading researcher on informal labour markets in South Asia. Her work quantifies the economic contribution of street vendors, domestic workers, and rickshaw drivers — the workforce that doesn't appear in GDP statistics.
input.show_description: A podcast about the economic activity that statistics miss. Audience: development economists, NGO workers, policy students. 4,500 downloads per episode.
input.why_this_guest: Her dataset of 45,000 informal workers across 12 Indian cities is the largest of its kind and challenges the ILO's standard measurement framework
input.specific_hook: What happens to an economy when you accurately count the people it pretends don't exist

input.guest_background: Climate scientist who appeared on the show 18 months ago discussing ocean acidification. Since then, he has shifted his research focus entirely to climate grief — the psychological impact on climate scientists who spend their careers documenting environmental destruction.
input.show_description: A science podcast that goes beyond the paper — exploring the human side of research. 8,000 downloads per episode.
input.why_this_guest: His first appearance was our highest-performing episode. His career pivot from hard science to the psychology of scientists is a story our audience will connect with deeply.
input.specific_hook: Why a climate scientist stopped studying the climate — and started studying what studying the climate does to people
input.previous_episode: Episode 47: 'The pH of Panic' — 23,000 downloads (our record)
